summary: Surya endpoint connectivity is confirmed. Customer-pack normalize now binds Surya as an optional fallback for degraded PDFs and low-confidence images, but remote OCR stays fail-closed unless the private draft has an approved non-default envelope with explicit remote egress policy and non-raw redaction state.

validation.command:
.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -m py_compile src\play_book_studio\intake\models.py src\play_book_studio\intake\private_boundary.py src\play_book_studio\app\intake_api.py src\play_book_studio\intake\private_corpus.py src\play_book_studio\intake
ormalization\degraded_pdf.py src\play_book_studio\intake
ormalization uilders.py src\play_book_studio\intake
ormalization\service.py src\play_book_studio\intake
ormalization\surya_adapter.py tests	est_intake_degraded_pdf.py tests	est_intake_multiformat.py tests	est_app_intake_ui.py tests	est_private_lane_smoke.py

remaining_risks: Remote Surya OCR still cannot fire for default-tenant/default-workspace or unreviewed drafts; those envelopes remain blocked by design.
Normal UI flows do not yet expose provider_egress_policy or redaction_state overrides, so approved remote OCR is currently an API/runtime contract rather than a surfaced product control.
